_metadata.chapter_title: Internal Morphology of the Spinal Cord and Brain in Stained Sections
_metadata.stain: Myelin (Weigert-type) and cell body stains; color overlays for fiber systems
_metadata.orientation_note: All sections shown in axial plane; superior is toward top of image in most figures; images are printed with inferior side up (atlas convention — brain viewed from below)

anatomical_levels.level_name: Forebrain — Mid-Thalamic / Basal Ganglia Level (superior cut through thalamus)
anatomical_levels.orientation: Axial, inferior aspect of thalamus, superior basal ganglia

tracts.somatotopy: head/body of caudate — dorsomedial to internal capsule
tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia motor circuit (indirect/direct pathway)
tracts.notes: Large C-shaped gray matter mass lateral to lateral ventricle body

tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia motor circuit
tracts.notes: Curves posteriorly and inferiorly around thalamus

tracts.somatotopy: leg dorsal, arm middle, face ventral within putamen
tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia motor circuit (direct and indirect)
tracts.notes: Lateral component of lentiform nucleus; separated from caudate by internal capsule anterior limb

tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia indirect pathway output
tracts.notes: Lateral pallidal segment; medial to putamen

tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia direct pathway output (to thalamus VL)
tracts.notes: Main output nucleus of basal ganglia; target for DBS in Parkinson disease

tracts.somatotopy: frontopontine fibers (lateral) and anterior thalamic radiations (medial); no strict somatotopy
tracts.clinical_pathway: frontopontine tract; anterior thalamic radiation (prefrontal-thalamic)
tracts.notes: Between caudate head (medially) and putamen/GP (laterally); contains frontopontine and anterior thalamic radiations

tracts.somatotopy: corticobulbar fibers for face (bilateral) in genu; head/face representation
tracts.vascular_territory: lenticulostriate arteries (primarily); small contribution from anterior choroidal
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor-Corticobulbar tract (to cranial nerve motor nuclei: VII, X, XII)
tracts.notes: Knee of internal capsule at level of genu; corticobulbar fibers most concentrated here

tracts.somatotopy: from anterior to posterior: face (genu transition) → arm → leg (most posterior); corticospinal fibers in middle third of posterior limb
tracts.vascular_territory: anterior two-thirds: lenticulostriate arteries; posterior third: anterior choroidal artery
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor-Corticospinal tract (face→arm→leg anterior to posterior); also sensory thalamocortical fibers (posterior portion)
tracts.notes: Most clinically important segment; small lacunar infarct here causes dense contralateral hemiplegia

tracts.somatotopy: optic radiation (inferior) and posterior thalamic radiation
tracts.clinical_pathway: optic tract/radiation (visual pathway); posterior thalamic radiation (somesthetic)
tracts.notes: Posterior to lentiform nucleus; contains optic radiation passing to occipital cortex

tracts.clinical_pathway: optic radiation (Meyer loop — inferior fibers for superior visual field); temporopontine fibers
tracts.notes: Below lentiform nucleus; Meyer loop of optic radiation passes here toward temporal lobe

tracts.somatotopy: leg lateral, arm middle, face medial (within VPL)
tracts.vascular_territory: thalamogeniculate branches of PCA; posterior choroidal artery
tracts.clinical_pathway: sensory-Medial lemniscus relay (contralateral body proprioception, discriminative touch); spinothalamic relay
tracts.notes: Primary somatosensory thalamic relay for body; projects to S1 (postcentral gyrus)

tracts.somatotopy: face/oral cavity somatotopy; trigeminal inputs
tracts.clinical_pathway: sensory-face relay (trigeminal lemniscus to somatosensory cortex face area)
tracts.notes: Relay for trigeminal (face) sensation; medial to VPL

tracts.somatotopy: leg/arm motor somatotopy from cerebellum/basal ganglia
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor — cerebellar and pallidal relay to motor cortex (M1); target for essential tremor DBS
tracts.notes: VLa receives GPi; VLp receives cerebellum (dentate); projects to precentral gyrus

tracts.clinical_pathway: prefrontal-limbic relay; reciprocal with prefrontal cortex
tracts.notes: Large nucleus in medial thalamus; bilateral infarct causes severe amnesia (Korsakoff)

tracts.clinical_pathway: limbic circuit (Papez circuit relay)
visual and multimodal association relay
tracts.notes: Largest thalamic nucleus; posterior thalamic pillow; multimodal

tracts.somatotopy: retinotopic: upper retina (lower field) medial; lower retina (upper field) lateral
tracts.vascular_territory: anterior choroidal artery (hilum) and posterior choroidal artery (peripheral)
tracts.clinical_pathway: optic tract/radiation — visual relay; optic tract → LGN → optic radiation → V1
tracts.notes: Dual blood supply makes LGN susceptible to characteristic pie-in-sky or pie-on-floor field defects with choroidal occlusion

tracts.somatotopy: tonotopic
tracts.clinical_pathway: auditory relay — inferior colliculus → MGN → auditory cortex (Heschl gyri)
hippocampal-mammillary-thalamic (Papez) circuit; memory
tracts.notes: Arches over thalamus; columns descend toward mammillary bodies

tracts.somatotopy: leg medial, arm lateral, face most lateral in body
tracts.clinical_pathway: interhemispheric transfer of motor and sensory information

tracts.zone: Cerebral white matter (superior to internal capsule)
tracts.somatotopy: fan mirrors internal capsule — leg medial/superior, arm middle, face lateral/inferior
tracts.vascular_territory: MCA (lateral corona radiata); ACA (medial corona radiata); watershed zone superiorly
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor-Corticospinal tract (superior portion); somatosensory thalamocortical radiation
tracts.notes: Superior continuation of internal capsule fibers; more dispersed; stroke here causes less dense deficits than internal capsule

tracts.clinical_pathway: limbic association tract; connects frontal, parietal, temporal limbic areas
claustrocortical connections; uncinate fasciculus passage
tracts.notes: Between putamen (medially) and claustrum (laterally)

tracts.clinical_pathway: language network (arcuate/superior longitudinal fasciculus ventral route); insular connections
tracts.notes: Between claustrum (medially) and insular cortex (laterally)

tracts.clinical_pathway: consciousness / cortico-cortical integration (proposed); not a defined clinical pathway

tracts.vascular_territory: periventricular branches (terminal zone) of MCA and ACA
tracts.clinical_pathway: CSF pathway; periventricular white matter adjacent carries motor fibers

anatomical_levels.level_name: Forebrain — Thalamus / Internal Capsule Level (mid-thalamic, at level of posterior commissure region)
anatomical_levels.orientation: Axial, through thalamus showing nuclei and internal capsule clearly with color-coded fiber systems

tracts.vascular_territory: lenticulostriate arteries (Heubner for head, lateral lenticulostriates for body)
tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia circuit

tracts.somatotopy: leg dorsal, arm central, face ventral
tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia motor circuit
basal ganglia output (GPi → VL thalamus via ansa lenticularis and lenticular fasciculus)
tracts.notes: Color-coded section shows pallidal fibers projecting medially

tracts.somatotopy: frontopontine (lateral fibers) and thalamocortical prefrontal (medial fibers)
tracts.clinical_pathway: frontopontine tract; anterior thalamic radiation

tracts.somatotopy: corticobulbar (head/face); most rostral motor fibers
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor-Corticobulbar tract
tracts.notes: Genu fibers for cranial nerve motor nuclei (pseudobulbar palsy if bilateral damage)

tracts.somatotopy: anterior to posterior: face → arm → leg (from genu to retrolenticular); arm fibers in anterior half of posterior limb
tracts.vascular_territory: anterior two-thirds: lenticulostriate; posterior third: anterior choroidal artery
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor-Corticospinal tract (primary motor fibers for contralateral limbs)
tracts.notes: Color-coded green in atlas; dense concentration of UMN fibers; lacunar infarct here = pure motor hemiplegia

tracts.tract_name: Internal capsule — posterior limb (thalamocortical sensory fibers)
tracts.somatotopy: body somatotopy mirrors VPL projection
tracts.vascular_territory: anterior choroidal artery (posterior segment of posterior limb)
tracts.clinical_pathway: sensory-Medial lemniscus (thalamocortical projection from VPL/VPM → S1)
tracts.notes: Color-coded blue in atlas

tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia output — GPi to VL thalamus (motor relay)
tracts.notes: Passes through/below zona incerta; joins ansa lenticularis at Forel H field

tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia output — GPi to VL thalamus (loops ventrally around internal capsule)
tracts.notes: Ventral pallidothalamic pathway

tracts.quadrant: Medial to internal capsule (inferior thalamic level)
tracts.somatotopy: sensorimotor (lateral STN), associative (medial STN)
tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia indirect pathway hub; target for Parkinson disease DBS
tracts.notes: Damage → contralateral hemiballismus

tracts.vascular_territory: polar artery (thalamotuberal — from posterior communicating or PCA)
tracts.clinical_pathway: Papez circuit relay (mammillary body → mammillothalamic tract → AN → cingulate)
tracts.notes: Part of limbic/memory circuit; damage contributes to amnesia

tracts.clinical_pathway: memory circuit (Papez) — mammillary body to anterior thalamus
tracts.notes: Damage (Wernicke encephalopathy) → anterograde amnesia

tracts.somatotopy: leg lateral, arm middle, face medial
tracts.clinical_pathway: sensory-Medial lemniscus and spinothalamic relay for body
tracts.notes: Color blue in atlas

tracts.somatotopy: retinotopic (6 layers)
tracts.clinical_pathway: visual pathway relay
tracts.notes: Visible as lateral bump of thalamus

tracts.somatotopy: upper retina (lower visual field) medial; lower retina (upper visual field) lateral; macular fibers central
tracts.clinical_pathway: optic tract/radiation — contralateral homonymous hemianopia if damaged
tracts.notes: Wraps around cerebral peduncle at this level

tracts.tract_name: Corticospinal fibers in cerebral peduncle (basis pedunculi)
tracts.somatotopy: face (corticobulbar) medial; arm middle; leg lateral in peduncle
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor-Corticospinal tract (descending to spinal cord) + motor-Corticobulbar
tracts.notes: Middle three-fifths of basis pedunculi carry corticospinal fibers

tracts.clinical_pathway: motor modulation; DBS target for tremor (caudal zona incerta)

anatomical_levels.level_name: Forebrain — Diencephalon / Mesencephalon Junction Level (at level of posterior commissure / superior colliculus transition)
anatomical_levels.orientation: Axial — transition from thalamus inferiorly; cerebellum and brainstem visible

tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia circuit

tracts.somatotopy: leg superior, arm middle, face inferior
tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia motor circuit
basal ganglia output

tracts.somatotopy: arm anterior, leg posterior in posterior limb
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor-Corticospinal tract; sensory thalamocortical

tracts.tract_name: Thalamus (multiple nuclei visible at this level)
tracts.clinical_pathway: multiple — sensory relay, motor relay, limbic
tracts.notes: VPL, VPM, and pulvinar visible at this level

tracts.somatotopy: retinotopic (6 laminae)
tracts.clinical_pathway: visual pathway relay

tracts.somatotopy: retinotopic (upper/lower retina)
tracts.clinical_pathway: visual — contralateral homonymous hemianopia with complete lesion
tracts.notes: Visible wrapping around cerebral peduncle

tracts.zone: Retrolenticular internal capsule / temporal-occipital white matter
tracts.somatotopy: upper field (Meyer loop, temporal) inferior; lower field parietal/superior
tracts.vascular_territory: MCA (temporal loop / Meyer loop); PCA (occipital portion)
tracts.clinical_pathway: visual pathway — optic radiation to primary visual cortex V1 (calcarine)
tracts.notes: Meyer loop takes inferior detour through temporal lobe — damaged in temporal lobectomy → contralateral superior quadrantanopia

tracts.somatotopy: medial: face/corticobulbar; middle: arm; lateral: leg
tracts.vascular_territory: posterior communicating artery perforators; anterior choroidal; PCA
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor-Corticospinal tract

tracts.somatotopy: leg lateral, arm medial (rotated from pons configuration)
tracts.clinical_pathway: sensory-Medial lemniscus (discriminative touch, proprioception, vibration from contralateral body)
tracts.notes: Approaches VPL thalamus from lateral tegmentum at this level

tracts.somatotopy: leg posterior/lateral, arm middle, face anterior/medial
tracts.clinical_pathway: sensory-Spinothalamic tract (pain, temperature, crude touch from contralateral body)
tracts.notes: Travels adjacent to medial lemniscus at mesencephalic level

tracts.clinical_pathway: dopaminergic nigrostriatal projection (degenerated in Parkinson disease)
tracts.notes: Appears dark in stained sections due to neuromelanin

tracts.clinical_pathway: rubrospinal tract; cerebellar dentate → red nucleus → thalamus relay
tracts.notes: Round nuclear mass in tegmentum; landmark for Benedikt syndrome (ipsilateral CN III + contralateral cerebellar tremor)

tracts.clinical_pathway: CSF drainage; periaqueductal gray = pain modulation, Parinaud syndrome if dorsal compression
pupillary light reflex pathway (pretectal nuclei coordination); damage → Argyll Robertson pupil / Parinaud
limbic-to-brainstem relay (habenulointerpeduncular tract = fasciculus retroflexus)

tracts.somatotopy: vermis=axial; hemispheres=limb
tracts.clinical_pathway: cerebellar motor coordination
tracts.notes: Visible in inferior portion of this forebrain-level section

tracts.clinical_pathway: MCA territory boundary landmark; language areas (Broca/Wernicke) adjacent

anatomical_levels.level_name: Forebrain — Thalamus Level (at level of anterior thalamic nuclei / mammillary bodies / superior colliculus visible)
anatomical_levels.orientation: Axial, mid-thalamic; mammillary bodies may be visible inferiorly

tracts.vascular_territory: recurrent artery of Heubner (head); medial lenticulostriate (body)
tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia circuit

tracts.somatotopy: leg superior, arm central, face inferior
tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia motor circuit
basal ganglia output to thalamus

tracts.somatotopy: anterior limb: frontopontine+ant thalamic rad; genu: corticobulbar (face); posterior limb: face→arm→leg; retrolenticular: optic radiation
tracts.vascular_territory: lenticulostriate (anterior portions); anterior choroidal (posterior limb posterior + retrolenticular)
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor (corticospinal, corticobulbar), sensory (thalamocortical), visual (optic radiation)
tracts.notes: Full bowtie shape visible at this level

tracts.clinical_pathway: prefrontal circuit; memory; emotion

tracts.somatotopy: VPL: leg lateral/arm/face medial; VPM: face
tracts.clinical_pathway: sensory relay — medial lemniscus and spinothalamic

tracts.somatotopy: retinotopic
tracts.clinical_pathway: visual pathway

tracts.vascular_territory: posterior communicating artery perforators; PCA (P1 segment)
tracts.clinical_pathway: memory circuit (Papez) — hippocampus → fornix → mammillary body → mammillothalamic tract → anterior thalamus
tracts.notes: Atrophied in Wernicke-Korsakoff syndrome

tracts.clinical_pathway: hippocampal-mammillary memory circuit
memory encoding and consolidation

tracts.somatotopy: corticobulbar medial, arm middle, leg lateral; frontopontine lateral fifth
tracts.vascular_territory: posterior communicating perforators; anterior choroidal; PCA

venous landmark; joins internal cerebral vein

tracts.somatotopy: frontopontine, anterior thalamic radiation
tracts.clinical_pathway: frontopontine tract; anterior thalamic radiation

tracts.somatotopy: corticobulbar (face/head representation)
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor-Corticobulbar tract

tracts.somatotopy: face (genu adjacent) → arm (anterior) → leg (posterior) — anterior to posterior
tracts.vascular_territory: anterior two-thirds: lenticulostriate; posterior third: anterior choroidal
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor-Corticospinal tract

tracts.somatotopy: optic radiation (upper/lower visual fields)
tracts.clinical_pathway: optic radiation (visual pathway)

tracts.somatotopy: leg lateral, arm middle, face medial
tracts.clinical_pathway: sensory-Medial lemniscus relay; spinothalamic relay
visual and multimodal association
arousal/consciousness; motor integration; DBS target for pain and consciousness disorders
thalamic gating/inhibition (GABAergic); modulates sensory relay
tracts.notes: Thin GABAergic shell separating thalamus from internal capsule

tracts.somatotopy: medial: leg; middle: arm; lateral: face (mirrors internal capsule)
tracts.vascular_territory: superior: ACA and MCA watershed; lateral: MCA; medial: ACA
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor-Corticospinal + sensory thalamocortical radiation
language (arcuate fasciculus component — Broca to Wernicke); spatial attention (SLF I/II)
amygdala → hypothalamus (limbic output pathway); anxiety circuits
interhemispheric olfactory and anterior temporal connections

tracts.tract_name: Substantia innominata / nucleus basalis of Meynert
tracts.clinical_pathway: cholinergic projection to cortex; degenerated in Alzheimer disease

anatomical_levels.level_name: Forebrain — Thalamus / Posterior Limb Internal Capsule Level (showing posterior limb in detail and surrounding structures)
anatomical_levels.orientation: Axial through thalamus; corona radiata to subcortical junction; clear posterior limb internal capsule

tracts.vascular_territory: recurrent artery of Heubner (ACA); medial lenticulostriate
tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia cognitive circuit

tracts.somatotopy: dorsal: leg; central: arm; ventral: face
tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia motor circuit
basal ganglia output

tracts.somatotopy: from genu going posteriorly: corticobulbar (face) → arm (corticospinal) → leg (corticospinal) → thalamocortical sensory fibers most posterior
tracts.vascular_territory: anterior 2/3: lenticulostriate; posterior 1/3: anterior choroidal artery
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor-Corticospinal tract (arm and leg); motor-Corticobulbar (face); sensory thalamocortical
tracts.notes: Most critical segment for motor function; posterior limb lesion = contralateral dense hemiplegia

tracts.somatotopy: visual (optic radiation) inferior; somatosensory superior
tracts.clinical_pathway: optic radiation; posterior thalamic radiation (somatosensory)

tracts.tract_name: Thalamus — VPL, VPM, LGN, MGN, pulvinar
tracts.somatotopy: VPL (body); VPM (face); LGN (vision); MGN (hearing); pulvinar (multimodal)
tracts.vascular_territory: thalamogeniculate (PCA) for VPL/VPM/pulvinar; anterior choroidal+posterior choroidal for LGN
tracts.clinical_pathway: sensory relay (VPL/VPM); visual relay (LGN); auditory relay (MGN)

tracts.somatotopy: inferior (Meyer loop through temporal lobe): upper retina/lower visual field; superior (parietal): lower retina/upper visual field; macular: central
tracts.vascular_territory: Meyer loop: MCA (anterior temporal); parietal loop: MCA (posterior parietal); occipital: PCA
tracts.clinical_pathway: optic radiation — LGN to primary visual cortex (V1 at calcarine sulcus); hemianopia / quadrantanopia depending on lesion location
tracts.notes: Complete lesion: contralateral homonymous hemianopia (macular sparing if PCA territory, because macula also supplied by MCA)

tracts.clinical_pathway: memory (declarative/episodic); mesial temporal lobe epilepsy focus

tracts.vascular_territory: anterior choroidal artery (temporal horn choroid plexus)
tracts.clinical_pathway: CSF production
visual cortex; memory; PCA stroke = contralateral homonymous hemianopia + amnesia (if bilateral)

anatomical_levels.level_name: Forebrain — Hypothalamus / Anterior Thalamus Level (at level of hypothalamus, amygdala, anterior internal capsule)
anatomical_levels.orientation: Axial through hypothalamus and anterior/mid-thalamus; hippocampus beginning; amygdala visible

tracts.vascular_territory: recurrent artery of Heubner; medial lenticulostriate
tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia (head of caudate — prefrontal/cognitive territory)

tracts.somatotopy: leg superior, arm middle, face inferior
tracts.clinical_pathway: basal ganglia motor circuit

tracts.somatotopy: anterior limb: frontopontine+ant thalamic radiation; genu: corticobulbar
tracts.clinical_pathway: frontopontine; anterior thalamic radiation; corticobulbar

tracts.vascular_territory: anterior choroidal artery; MCA (anterior temporal branches)
tracts.clinical_pathway: fear/emotional processing; uncinate fasciculus connections; temporal lobe epilepsy
tracts.notes: Anterior to hippocampus; medial to lateral ventricle temporal horn

tracts.vascular_territory: anterior choroidal artery (CA1 sector); posterior communicating perforators
tracts.clinical_pathway: declarative memory; CA1 vulnerable to ischemia

tracts.vascular_territory: posterior communicating artery perforators; anterior communicating artery perforators (anterior hypothalamus)
tracts.clinical_pathway: autonomic regulation; endocrine (pituitary); temperature regulation; sleep-wake
tracts.notes: Lateral hypothalamic syndrome: ipsilateral Horner (hypothalamospinal tract)

tracts.somatotopy: upper field fibers (lower retina) lateral; lower field (upper retina) medial
tracts.clinical_pathway: visual pathway — contralateral homonymous hemianopia with complete lesion
interhemispheric olfactory and temporal lobe connections
tracts.notes: Visible at this level as compact bundle

tracts.clinical_pathway: frontal-temporal connection; language (naming); social/emotional behavior; damaged in semantic dementia
tracts.notes: Hooks under Sylvian fissure connecting orbitofrontal to temporal pole

tracts.clinical_pathway: visual recognition / object identification pathway; prosopagnosia if bilateral
MCA territory boundary; language cortices adjacent

tracts.vascular_territory: posterior communicating artery perforators; lateral hypothalamic branches
tracts.clinical_pathway: sympathetic pathway (descending to ciliospinal center of Budge in C8-T1 lateral horn); lesion = ipsilateral Horner syndrome
tracts.notes: Runs through lateral tegmentum from hypothalamus to T1 spinal cord

anatomical_levels.level_name: Forebrain — Optic Tract / Hypothalamus Level (inferior, at chiasm vicinity; temporal lobe; deep to cortex)
anatomical_levels.orientation: Axial through hypothalamus/optic tract; inferior basal forebrain; temporal and frontal lobes at this level

tracts.somatotopy: nasal (decussating) fibers cross at chiasm; temporal fibers ipsilateral; after chiasm: contralateral homonymous fields
tracts.vascular_territory: anterior communicating artery (superior chiasm); ophthalmic artery / internal carotid (inferior chiasm); anterior choroidal (optic tract)
tracts.clinical_pathway: visual pathway — chiasm lesion: bitemporal hemianopia; optic tract: contralateral homonymous hemianopia
tracts.notes: Superior chiasm supplied by ACA; inferior by ICA; pituitary tumor classically compresses chiasm from below

tracts.somatotopy: 6 laminae — monocular layers 1,4,6 vs 2,3,5; upper field medial, lower field lateral
tracts.vascular_territory: anterior choroidal artery (hilum/inferior) + posterior lateral choroidal (peripheral/superior)
tracts.clinical_pathway: visual relay — lesion causes sectoranopia (wedge scotoma) due to dual blood supply

tracts.zone: Brainstem (medial tegmentum — visible at this level in lower diencephalon/midbrain transition)
tracts.vascular_territory: paramedian branches of basilar artery / PCA
tracts.clinical_pathway: conjugate gaze — internuclear ophthalmoplegia if damaged (MLF lesion between PPRF-CN VI and CN III)
autonomic / neuroendocrine

tracts.somatotopy: medial: face (corticobulbar); arm: middle; leg: lateral
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor-Corticospinal + motor-Corticobulbar

tracts.somatotopy: leg lateral/posterior, arm middle, face medial/anterior
tracts.clinical_pathway: sensory-Medial lemniscus (contralateral discriminative touch / proprioception)

tracts.somatotopy: leg posterior, arm middle, face anterior
tracts.clinical_pathway: sensory-Spinothalamic tract (pain and temperature from contralateral body)
memory
memory consolidation; Alzheimer disease early affected; olfactory input relay

tracts.quadrant: Lateral temporal / deep to extreme capsule
tracts.clinical_pathway: semantic language network; frontal-occipital connections
visceral sensation; pain; vestibular; language; autonomic
olfactory cortex (primary); seizure propagation in TLE

anatomical_levels.level_name: Forebrain — Amygdala / Temporal Horn Level (at inferior temporal lobe, amygdala, basal forebrain)
anatomical_levels.orientation: Axial through amygdala and temporal horn of lateral ventricle; inferior basal ganglia level

tracts.vascular_territory: anterior choroidal artery; MCA anterior temporal branches
tracts.clinical_pathway: fear/emotional processing; Kluver-Bucy syndrome if bilateral; TLE focus
tracts.notes: Anterior to hippocampus at this level

tracts.clinical_pathway: declarative memory; episodic memory

CSF; enlarges in mesial temporal sclerosis (atrophy of hippocampus)

tracts.zone: Temporal lobe white matter (lateral and inferior to temporal horn)
tracts.somatotopy: upper retinal fibers (lower visual field) — most anterior loop

tracts.clinical_pathway: visual object recognition; prosopagnosia; semantic memory

tracts.zone: Along thalamocaudate groove / temporal horn medial wall
tracts.clinical_pathway: amygdala → hypothalamus / BNST (bed nucleus stria terminalis); anxiety and stress circuits

tracts.vascular_territory: ACA (olfactory artery from A1); MCA (anterior temporal)

cortico-cortical integration (consciousness proposed)

anatomical_levels.level_name: Vascular Territory Map — Multiple Forebrain Levels (ACA / MCA / PCA / lenticulostriate / anterior choroidal territories)
anatomical_levels.orientation: Composite axial diagram illustrating arterial territories across 3-4 forebrain levels (rostral to caudal)
anatomical_levels.notes: This figure is specifically a vascular territory map — color-coded regions for each arterial territory at each forebrain level

structures_supplied: Medial frontal cortex (paracentral lobule — leg area of M1 and S1)
Corpus callosum (pericallosal artery — body and splenium)
Medial corona radiata (leg fibers of corticospinal tract)
Anterior limb of internal capsule (medial portion — recurrent artery of Heubner)
clinical_syndromes: ACA stroke: contralateral leg weakness > arm (paracentral lobule); abulia; urinary incontinence; alien hand (callosal involvement)
Heubner infarct: caudate head + anterior limb IC → mild contralateral weakness + behavioral changes

structures_supplied: Lateral frontal cortex (inferior, middle frontal gyri — Broca area in dominant hemisphere)
Lateral temporal cortex (superior temporal gyrus — Wernicke area in dominant hemisphere)
Primary motor cortex (arm and face areas — precentral gyrus lateral)
Primary somatosensory cortex (arm and face — postcentral gyrus lateral)
clinical_syndromes: Complete MCA: dense contralateral hemiplegia (arm > leg), hemisensory loss, hemianopia, aphasia (dominant) or neglect (non-dominant)
Superior division MCA: motor + Broca aphasia
Inferior division MCA: Wernicke aphasia + hemianopia

structures_supplied: Lateral lenticulostriate: putamen, GPe, anterior 2/3 of posterior limb of IC, anterior limb of IC (lateral portion), caudate body
Medial lenticulostriate: caudate head (medial), anterior limb IC (medial)
clinical_syndromes: Lacunar infarct — posterior limb IC: pure motor hemiplegia (face+arm+leg equally affected)
Putaminal hemorrhage (hypertensive): contralateral hemiplegia, hemisensory loss, hemianopia
Lacunar infarct — anterior limb IC/caudate: mild motor + behavioral changes

structures_supplied: Posterior limb of internal capsule (posterior 1/3 — where thalamocortical sensory fibers travel)
clinical_syndromes: AChA syndrome (classic triad): contralateral hemiplegia (posterior limb IC motor fibers) + hemisensory loss + hemianopia (optic radiation / LGN)
Variable presentation due to extensive collaterals; may be partial
Anterior choroidal stroke: pure motor stroke if only posterior limb IC involved; LGN infarct: sectoranopia (wedge)

structures_supplied: Primary visual cortex V1 (calcarine cortex) — entire cortical visual processing
Visual association cortex (V2, V3, V4, V5 in occipital and occipitotemporal cortex)
Inferior and medial temporal cortex (parahippocampal gyrus, fusiform gyrus posterior, lingual gyrus)
Thalamic nuclei (thalamogeniculate arteries: VPL, VPM, pulvinar, centromedian)
Thalamoperforating arteries (posterior): mediodorsal thalamus, intralaminar nuclei, subthalamus, STN
Posterior choroidal artery: LGN (peripheral), MGN, pulvinar, posterior thalamus, choroid plexus (body/atrium)
Midbrain tegmentum (short and long circumferential branches)
Posterior hypothalamus (mammillary bodies via P1 perforators)
clinical_syndromes: PCA cortical: contralateral homonymous hemianopia (macular sparing common — macular cortex also MCA supplied)
PCA with thalamic: hemianopia + hemisensory loss (Dejerine-Roussy syndrome if thalamic pain)
Bilateral PCA: cortical blindness (Anton syndrome — denial of blindness)
P1 perforators: top-of-the-basilar syndrome — bilateral thalamic + midbrain infarct → coma, vertical gaze palsy
Splenium + left occipital: alexia without agraphia (dominant hemisphere PCA)

structures_supplied: Anterior thalamus (polar artery — thalamotuberal artery: anterior thalamus, mammillothalamic tract)
clinical_syndromes: Bilateral polar artery infarcts (from single thalamotuberal artery arising from one PComm): severe anterograde amnesia (Korsakoff-like without alcohol history — 'strategic infarct amnesia')

vascular_territories.zone: Parasagittal cortex and posterior parieto-occipital cortex
vascular_territories.somatotopy: ACA-MCA watershed: shoulder/arm area (proximal limb weakness in hypoperfusion — 'man in a barrel')
vascular_territories.clinical_pathway: vulnerable to hypoperfusion — border zone infarcts; proximal arm and leg weakness with face/hand sparing (ACA-MCA); visual dysfunction (MCA-PCA)
vascular_territories.notes: Visible on p-36 as transition zones between colored vascular territories

tracts.zone: Motor cortex → corona radiata → internal capsule → cerebral peduncle
tracts.somatotopy: face lateral/genu; arm anterior posterior limb; leg posterior in posterior limb; in corona radiata: leg medial, arm middle, face lateral
tracts.vascular_territory: cortex: MCA (arm/face) + ACA (leg); corona radiata: MCA (lateral) + ACA (medial); IC posterior limb: lenticulostriate (anterior 2/3) + AChA (posterior 1/3); peduncle: posterior communicating + PCA
tracts.clinical_pathway: motor-Corticospinal tract — complete map across all forebrain levels shown
tracts.notes: Single artery (lenticulostriate or AChA) occlusion in posterior limb IC can produce maximal motor deficit equal to entire MCA cortical territory

tracts.zone: Thalamus VPL → posterior limb IC → corona radiata → postcentral gyrus
tracts.somatotopy: VPL: leg lateral, arm middle, face medial → posterior limb IC: similar arrangement → S1 postcentral
tracts.vascular_territory: VPL: thalamogeniculate (PCA); posterior limb IC (sensory portion): AChA; corona radiata: MCA; S1 cortex: MCA (arm/face) + ACA (leg)
tracts.clinical_pathway: sensory-Medial lemniscus (discriminative touch / proprioception) — thalamocortical portion

tracts.zone: Optic tract → LGN → optic radiation → V1
tracts.somatotopy: lower retina/upper field superior (parietal loop); upper retina/lower field inferior (Meyer loop temporal); macular central
tracts.vascular_territory: optic tract: AChA; LGN: AChA (hilum) + posterior choroidal (peripheral); Meyer loop: MCA (anterior temporal); parietal loop: MCA posterior parietal; calcarine V1: PCA
tracts.clinical_pathway: optic tract/radiation — visual field deficits map predictably to lesion location
tracts.notes: Macular sparing in PCA occipital infarct because macular V1 has dual supply (MCA + PCA)

internal_capsule_summary.description: Detailed somatotopic and vascular map of the internal capsule — synthesized across all forebrain sections

segments.somatotopy: No strict motor somatotopy; frontopontine fibers lateral, thalamic radiations medial
segments.vascular_territory: Medial lenticulostriate (from MCA M1); lateral lenticulostriate (from MCA M1)
segments.clinical: Lacunar infarct: mild contralateral weakness + personality/behavioral changes; often subclinical

contents: Corticobulbar fibers (face representation — bilateral for most cranial nerves, contralateral for lower face VII)
segments.somatotopy: Face / head — corticobulbar for CN VII (lower face contralateral), IX, X, XI, XII
segments.clinical: Genu lacune: contralateral central facial weakness; if bilateral: pseudobulbar palsy (dysarthria, dysphagia, emotional lability)

segments.somatotopy: Face (genu junction) → arm (primary location for arm corticospinal)
segments.clinical: Lacunar infarct: contralateral face+arm weakness (pure motor stroke)

contents: Corticospinal (arm and leg fibers — most concentrated motor fibers)
segments.vascular_territory: Lateral lenticulostriate arteries (MCA) — anterior; junction with AChA posteriorly
segments.clinical: Densest lacunar pure motor stroke location; small (<15mm) infarct = complete contralateral hemiplegia
AChA infarct: hemiplegia + hemisensory loss; thalamocortical involvement adds sensory component

segments.somatotopy: Upper visual field inferior (Meyer loop); lower visual field superior (parietal loop); macular central
segments.vascular_territory: Anterior choroidal artery (predominantly); PCA posterior choroidal (contribution)
segments.clinical: AChA infarct: adds homonymous hemianopia to classic triad

contents: Meyer loop of optic radiation (anterior loop through temporal lobe)
segments.somatotopy: Upper retinal fibers (lower visual field) — most anterior Meyer loop
segments.vascular_territory: Anterior choroidal artery; MCA (anterior temporal branches)
segments.clinical: Temporal lobectomy for epilepsy removes Meyer loop → contralateral superior quadrantanopia ('pie in the sky')

thalamic_nuclei_summary.description: Thalamic nuclei visible across forebrain sections — with clinical correlations

nuclei.sensory_modality: Contralateral body: discriminative touch, proprioception (medial lemniscus), pain/temperature (spinothalamic)
nuclei.somatotopy: Leg lateral, arm central, face medial
nuclei.projection: Primary somatosensory cortex S1 (postcentral gyrus, areas 3a/3b/1/2)
nuclei.clinical: VPL infarct: contralateral hemisensory loss; may develop into thalamic pain (Dejerine-Roussy syndrome)

nuclei.sensory_modality: Contralateral face: all modalities via trigeminal lemniscus
nuclei.projection: S1 face area (inferior postcentral gyrus); also gustatory cortex
nuclei.clinical: VPM infarct: contralateral facial sensory loss; combined VPL+VPM → pure sensory stroke

nuclei.sensory_modality: Motor relay (not sensory); VLa: basal ganglia input; VLp: cerebellar input
nuclei.projection: Primary motor cortex M1 (precentral gyrus); premotor cortex
nuclei.clinical: VLp: DBS target for essential tremor; VLa: related to pallidal circuit

nuclei.vascular_territory: Thalamoperforating arteries (paramedian from PCA or posterior communicating)
nuclei.clinical: Bilateral MD infarct: severe amnesia, apathy (Korsakoff-like); paramedian thalamic syndrome
Papez circuit relay; AN stimulation used for treatment-resistant epilepsy (SANTE trial)

nuclei.somatotopy: Retinotopic (6 laminae; upper field medial, lower field lateral)
nuclei.projection: Primary visual cortex V1 (calcarine cortex) via optic radiation
nuclei.clinical: LGN infarct: characteristic sectoranopia (wedge-shaped scotoma); pie-in-the-sky or pie-on-the-floor depending on which blood supply affected

nuclei.projection: Primary auditory cortex (Heschl gyri, superior temporal plane)
nuclei.clinical: MGN infarct: rarely causes clinically evident hearing loss (bilateral cortical representation)

nuclei.projection: Visual association cortex (V4, V5); parietal cortex; temporal cortex
nuclei.clinical: Pulvinar involvement in fatal familial insomnia; large posterior thalamic strokes affect spatial attention

circuits.pathway: Striatum (D1 MSN) → GPi/SNr → (inhibits) VL thalamus → motor cortex
circuits.clinical: Underactive in Parkinson (loss of dopaminergic D1 stimulation)

circuits.pathway: Striatum (D2 MSN) → GPe → STN → GPi/SNr → VL thalamus → motor cortex
circuits.relevant_nuclei: Caudate, putamen, GPe, STN, GPi, VL thalamus
circuits.clinical: Hyperactive in Parkinson (dopaminergic D2 disinhibition); STN lesion → hemiballismus

vascular_territories_basal_ganglia.caudate_head: Recurrent artery of Heubner (ACA) + medial lenticulostriate (MCA)
